group.label: Runtime Safeguards
group.comment:
AI requests can take several seconds to complete. If too many run at once, or if individual requests run too long, the rest of the site can slow down or become unresponsive.
Use these settings to limit how many AI requests run at the same time and how long each one may run.

field.label: AI Request Timeout (seconds)
field.comment:
This specifies how long a single AI request is allowed to run. If the request exceeds this time, it will be stopped (best-effort; only applies where supported by the provider implementation).

Choose a value that aligns with your AI requirements. Keep in mind your web server and your CDN (e.g. Nginx, Apache, or Cloudflare) may have their own timeout settings. It's best to use the same value or a lower one so that this limit is the one that applies.

Set to 0 to disable this setting and use system default.

field.label: Max Concurrent AI Requests
field.comment:
How many AI requests can run at the same time. This setting uses the Magento locking system to keep total AI load in check so the rest of the site stays responsive. For more information, see the Adobe Commerce lock provider documentation.

Set to 0 to disable the limit.
